description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, weathered surface and a muted beige coloration. The stone features a prominent notch or depression on one side, giving it a distinctive, almost saddle-like profile. Its surfaces are uneven, with some faces appearing more angular and blocky, while others are more rounded and eroded. Subtle white streaks and small patches of discoloration are visible, suggesting mineral inclusions or surface deposits. The stone's texture is coarse, with visible pitting and granular details typical of natural rock found in outdoor urban environments. Its overall form suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in parks, sidewalks, or landscaped urban spaces.
description: A large, rough beige stone with an irregular, blocky shape and a distinctive saddle-like depression on one side. Its coarse, weathered surface features subtle white streaks and granular texture.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's blocky, angular face with visible vertical grooves and a slightly protruding upper section.
description_view_1: The saddle-like depression is most prominent here, with the stone's surface curving inward and rough, eroded edges visible.
description_view_2: A broad, flat face dominates this view, showing subtle surface pitting and a more uniform beige coloration.
description_view_3: The deep central notch is clearly visible, with two raised sections on either side and a rough, granular texture throughout.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle, irregular streaks and granular pitting with no regular pattern; some faint white mineral lines.
attribute_weathering: Rough and eroded, with visible pitting and minor discoloration indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements or contrasts with paved surfaces; may be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
